title: CBA rout signals end of banks’ era on ASX: VanEck

summary: A report from VanEck suggests that the recent drop in Commonwealth Bank of Australia's (CBA) share price may indicate a decline in the dominance of banks on the Australian Stock Exchange (ASX). This change could impact investors and the overall market outlook for financial institutions.
heading: Banking and Economy
key_points: CBA's share price has fallen significantly.
VanEck believes this signals a shift away from bank dominance on the ASX.
The report may affect investor confidence in financial stocks.
ticker_line: CBA drop hints at banks' declining ASX power
